agent1_snippets.title: Pioglitazone -- Contraindications
agent1_snippets.content: Patients with a known hypersensitivity to pioglitazone or any of its components Diabetic ketoacidosis or type 1 diabetes, since pioglitazone is active only in the presence of insulin. Hypoglycemia, therefore, regular blood sugar monitoring is imperative Initiation of therapy in patients with established New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class III or IV heart failure is a contraindication in the US Symptomatic heart failure at any stage (NYHA Class I, II, III, IV) is contraindicated in Canada Any evidence of fluid overload Active (current) bladder cancer or history of bladder cancer, uninvestigated microscopic hematuria History of fracture or at high risk for fracture Active liver disease, liver transaminase  levels more than 2.5 times above the upper limit of normal Pregnancy The Beers Criteria lists pioglitazone as a potentially inappropriate medication (PIM) for the geriatric population with heart or kidney failure.

agent1_snippets.title: Insulinoma -- History and Physical
agent1_snippets.content: Fasting hypoglycemia is the most characteristic finding of insulinoma, reported in approximately 73% of cases. About 20% of patients experience both fasting and postprandial hypoglycemic symptoms. Additionally, an increasing number of patients have been reporting exclusively postprandial hypoglycemic symptoms, which appear to be more common in men. Weight gain is a common finding in most patients with insulinoma. [13]

agent1_snippets.title: Factitious Hypoglycemia -- History and Physical
agent1_snippets.content: Taking a good history remains a crucial step in evaluating hypoglycemic patients. Factitious hypoglycemia should be a potential diagnosis in the differential for patients who work in the medical profession, who are in close contact with diabetic individuals, and those with underlying psychiatric disorders like major depressive disorder and a history of suicide attempts. It is also essential to review the patient's current medication list, including any herbal preparations, as some herbals may be contaminated with sulfonylureas. It should also be suspected in diabetic individuals who, despite repeated adjustments of their insulin or oral hypoglycemic doses, continue to present with recurrent hypoglycemia episodes.

agent2_snippets.content: Glibenklamid and glimepiride are both members of the sulfonylurea class of oral agents used for blood glucose lowering. agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2721
agent2_snippets.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, اعتلال عصبي سكري, فرط أنسولين الدم, and ورم غلوكاغوني. ارتفاع السكر في الدم (high blood sugar) is a primary manifestation of diabetes, reflecting impaired glucose regulation by the endocrine pancreas. اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy) is a common chronic complication of prolonged hyperglycemia, affecting peripheral nerves and leading to sensory and motor deficits. فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia) denotes excessive insulin secretion, which can occur in early insulin resistance or insulin‑producing tumors and may precede or accompany diabetes. ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma) is a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, often resulting in persistent hyperglycemia and weight loss. Recognizing how these conditions are interrelated within endocrine pancreatic disease supports comprehensive diagnosis, monitoring, and therapeutic strategies for affected patients.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1875
agent2_snippets.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes several related disorders: ارتفاع السكر في الدم (elevated blood glucose), مرض السكري دون مضاعفات (diabetes without complications), اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy), فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia), and ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma). These entities represent a spectrum of pancreatic endocrine dysfunction, ranging from simple hyperglycemia to specific complications such as neuropathy, as well as hormonal excess states like hyperinsulinemia and glucagon‑secreting tumors. Understanding that all of these conditions are subtypes of diseases of the endocrine pancreas, including diabetes, helps clinicians recognize the varied presentations, anticipate potential complications, and tailor comprehensive patient care.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1930
agent2_snippets.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad classification that encompasses several distinct conditions. Within this category, مرض السكري دون مضاعفات refers to diabetes without complications, representing a form of the disease where hyperglycemia is present but secondary complications have not yet developed. فرط أنسولين الدم denotes hyperinsulinemia, a state of elevated insulin levels in the blood that can arise from pancreatic beta‑cell overactivity or insulin‑secreting tumors and may lead to hypoglycemia. ورم غلوكاغوني describes glucagonoma, a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, causing characteristic metabolic disturbances such as glucose intolerance, weight loss, and a distinctive skin rash. agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1756
agent2_snippets.content: The category “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” encompasses a spectrum of disorders and clinical manifestations that arise from dysfunction of the pancreatic endocrine system. It includes “ارتفاع السكر في الدم” (hyperglycemia), which represents elevated blood‑glucose levels and is a primary feature of diabetes. “غيبوبة فرط الأسمولية السكري” (diabetic hyperosmolar coma) is a severe, acute complication of uncontrolled hyperglycemia characterized by extreme dehydration and altered mental status. “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” (diabetes mellitus without complications) denotes diabetes in its uncomplicated form, serving as the baseline condition from which other sequelae may develop. Among the chronic complications, “اعتلال عصبي سكري” (diabetic neuropathy) reflects nerve damage caused by prolonged hyperglycemia, leading to sensory and motor deficits. “ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي” (progressive hemifacial atrophy) is listed as a possible extra‑pancreatic manifestation that can be associated with underlying endocrine pancreatic disease. Metabolic dysregulation is further illustrated by “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia), an excess of circulating insulin that may precede or accompany insulin resistance. Finally, “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma) represents a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, producing a distinct clinical syndrome. Together, these entities illustrate the diverse acute, chronic, and neoplastic conditions linked to diseases of the endocrine pancreas, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management of both glycemic control and associated complications.
